medications: Ferrous sulfate 325 mg (65 mg elemental iron) once daily
Cetirizine 10 mg as needed during allergy season
Fluticasone nasal spray 50 mcg per spray, 1–2 sprays per nostril daily as needed
Famotidine 20 mg as needed for reflux
health.mental_health: High stress and anxiety related to potential eviction and unstable sleep; worries about keeping work performance steady and affording groceries/medications. No formal diagnosis documented, but she has considered counseling through an employee assistance program.
recent_visits: Primary care visit at Straub Clinic (Jan 2026): fatigue and labs confirming low ferritin; iron plan and follow-up labs in 8–10 weeks
MinuteClinic/urgent care (Nov 2025): severe allergy flare; advised daily nasal steroid during peak season
Telehealth consult (Feb 2026): sleep issues and stress management; discussed CBT-I resources

finance.debt: About $18k in federal student loans; ~$3.4k on a credit card from moving costs, past-due utilities, and covering rent shortfalls. Currently 2 weeks behind on rent and has received a written notice from her landlord/management company.
investments: Small Roth IRA (opened in college, minimal balance)
recent_transactions: Partial rent payment to property manager (last week) with a remaining balance
Grocery run at Foodland with a reduced cart and use of discount points
One-time cash transfer from older sister to cover utilities
Prescription purchase: iron supplement and allergy meds
Bus pass/top-up after cutting back on rideshares

relationships: Older sister (Minji Kim, 28, lives in Kapolei) who sometimes helps financially and is urging her to move in temporarily
Roommate/ex-roommate (Leilani Paikaʻi, 23) who moved out unexpectedly, leaving Fatima with a larger share of rent
Mother (Aisha Rahman, lives on the mainland) with whom she has a complicated relationship and limited financial support
Close friend (Maya Tanaka, 22) who knows about the eviction risk and helps her look for short-term housing
On-and-off dating situation (Jordan K., 24) who doesn’t know the full extent of her housing instability
social.social_media_activity: Keeps Instagram mostly curated (beach photos, work coffee spots, hikes) and avoids posting anything about housing or money. Uses a private Close Friends list to vent occasionally. More active in local Discord/Reddit groups for Oʻahu housing and entry-level analytics careers under a pseudonym.
private_conversations: Texting her sister about a possible short-term couch arrangement and whether it would affect her sister’s lease
Messaging her landlord/property manager to negotiate a payment plan and avoid filing in court
Talking with a friend about feeling ashamed and hiding the situation from coworkers
Discussing whether to sell her laptop/tablet or pause medical follow-ups to afford rent

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri, 8:30am–5:30pm HST; month-end reporting can push to 7:00pm and occasional Saturday morning checks
regular_appointments: Weekly budgeting and housing search block (Sunday evenings)
Monthly allergy management routine (refill reminders; increases during spring)
Bi-monthly lab follow-up scheduling for anemia (as ordered, not always completed on time)
upcoming_events: Follow-up lab work for ferritin/CBC at Straub Clinic (scheduled for mid-March 2026)
Meeting with property manager to discuss payment plan and avoid eviction filing (next week)
Interview call for a higher-paying analyst role at a Waikīkī-area hospitality company (in 10 days)
Friend’s birthday dinner in Kakaʻako (tentative, depends on money)
schedule.daily_routine: Commutes by TheBus when possible, works in-office 3 days/week and remote 2 days/week; often skips breakfast, takes iron with water/food when she remembers, and tries to fit in a short walk after work. Evenings are split between job applications, apartment searches, and catching up on sleep.

professional.company: Mid-sized healthcare services contractor supporting clinics on Oʻahu (claims and operations analytics)
projects: Monthly KPI dashboard refresh in Power BI for clinic operations
Data quality checks on appointment no-show and billing codes
Building a simple forecast model for staffing needs using historical visit volumes
Ad-hoc analysis for leadership on patient wait times and scheduling bottlenecks
professional.performance_issues: Recently had two late deliverables due to unstable Wi‑Fi and disrupted sleep; requested to work more in-office to ensure connectivity. She is worried her manager will notice increased errors and that missing rent could force an unplanned move affecting her reliability.
workplace_relationships: Manager (Erin Nakamura) is supportive but expects consistent deadlines; Fatima hasn’t disclosed eviction risk
Senior analyst mentor (Kai Lono) helps with modeling and code reviews; Fatima sometimes asks to use a quiet conference room for calls
Friendly but superficial relationships with coworkers; she avoids after-work spending-heavy outings

hobbies: Beach walks and swimming (Ala Moana Beach Park)
Hiking easy trails (Makapuʻu Lighthouse when time allows)
Cooking simple Korean-inspired meals (jjigae, rice bowls) when she has a kitchen
Casual PC gaming and watching K-dramas
habits: Tracks expenses daily in a spreadsheet and checks bank balance multiple times a day
Keeps a packed 'go bag' with documents (ID, pay stubs, lease, lab orders) in case she has to move quickly
Uses free Wi‑Fi at the library or at work when home internet is unreliable
Skips meals occasionally to stretch groceries; relies on instant noodles and rice when stressed
Avoids inviting friends over due to embarrassment about her housing situation
lifestyle.living_situation: Renting a small studio near Moʻiliʻili; roommate moved out and the lease burden shifted. She is at risk of eviction after falling behind on rent and is quietly exploring temporary options (staying with her sister in Kapolei or a short-term sublet) while trying to keep her job stable.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked and budget-driven; tries to increase iron intake (spinach, beans, fortified cereal, occasional local beef) but often defaults to quick carbs when exhausted. Limits dairy around iron doses and drinks coffee, sometimes too close to taking supplements.
